setup.tip: Start every project with claude init to auto-generate a CLAUDE.md scaffold based on your repo.
Use claude --model claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 for quick boilerplate tasks — it's 10× faster and cheaper.
setup.example: claude --model claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 "generate getters and setters for User.ts"

setup.tip: Authenticate once with OAuth and never worry about token rotation. Requires Pro/Max/Team plan.
Pin your Node.js version in .nvmrc so Claude always uses the same runtime as your project.
Run claude --version to confirm your install, then claude update to get the latest.
The ~/.claude/settings.json file sets your global defaults across all projects — set your preferred model there.
Use claude --print flag for non-interactive output — great for piping Claude's response to a file or another command.

practices.tip: Store MEMORY.md per project in ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/ — Claude reads it at every session start and updates it automatically. Organise by topic, not date.
practices.example:
## Architecture
We use server components by default. Client components only when state is needed.

practices.tip: Name your Skills with action verbs (ReviewPR.md, RunMigration.md) — Claude triggers them more reliably than noun-based names.
Use deny: ["Bash(rm -rf*)", "Bash(git push --force*)"] in settings.json — block destructive commands at the permission layer, not just in CLAUDE.md.
Give agents the minimum tools they need. A code-reviewer only needs Read, Glob, Grep — never Write or Bash. Least-privilege by default.
Run /compact before switching tasks in the same session — saves 5k–20k tokens and keeps Claude focused on the new task only.
practices.example: /compact "auth bug fixed, now starting payment flow"

practices.tip: Use parallel sub-agents for independent work — launch multiple Agent calls in one message. Each runs simultaneously, reducing wall-clock time by 3-5x on parallelisable tasks.
practices.example: # Claude issues multiple Agent tool calls in a single message

practices.tip: Scope MCP filesystem servers to specific directories only — never grant root access. Add allowedDirectories in the server config.

news.tip: Claude 4.6 family is live: Opus 4.6 for reasoning, Sonnet 4.6 as the new Claude Code default, Haiku 4.5 for fast tasks. Set in ~/.claude/settings.json.
Fast mode (/fast) streams Opus 4.6 at higher throughput with no model downgrade — ideal for large file writes and repetitive refactoring sessions.
The status line shows live model, token count, cost and directory in the terminal — updated after every tool call without needing /status.
news.example: # Configure in ~/.claude/settings.json under the statusLine key

news.tip: Auto-memory (MEMORY.md) persists project context across sessions automatically — no more re-explaining architecture at the start of every conversation.
Parallel sub-agents are now supported — Claude launches multiple agents simultaneously in one response, cutting wall-clock time 3-5x for independent tasks.
news.example: # Claude issues multiple Agent tool calls in a single message

news.tip: Worktree isolation gives each sub-agent its own git branch to work safely — auto-cleaned if no changes are made.
The MEMORY.md + Skills two-layer pattern: MEMORY.md = what Claude knows, Skills = what Claude does. Together they eliminate session warm-up time entirely.
